publications.title: Structural basis for oligoclonal T cell recognition of a shared p53 cancer neoantigen.
publications.authors: Wu D, Gallagher DT, Gowthaman R, Pierce BG, Mariuzza RA.
publications.executive_summary: This study examined oligoclonal T cell receptors (TCRs) that recognize a shared neoantigen derived from a p53R175H driver mutation presented by HLA-A2, determining crystal structures of three distinct tumor-specific TCRs in complex with mutant p53-HLA-A2 and comparing them to wild-type p53-HLA-A2. The structural analysis revealed how the p53 point mutation rendered an otherwise self-peptide immunogenic and showed that despite their different binding modes, the three TCRs employ focused, structurally distinct strategies to discriminate between mutant and wild-type p53 epitopes. Because the p53R175H neoantigen is shared across multiple patients, understanding the molecular basis of TCR recognition of this mutation has direct implications for designing improved T cell therapies: the structures provide a rational foundation for engineering TCRs with enhanced potency and specificity for adoptive cell therapy against p53-driven cancers.
